title: Chittoor and Sri Sathya Sai districts issue heatwave advisory - The Hindu
full_text:
The Sri Sathya Sai and Chittoor district administrations have issued a heatwave advisory on Sunday (April 19, 2026), cautioning people about severe conditions ahead.
Sri Sathya Sai district Collector A. Shyam Prasad appealed to residents to follow the basic precautions issued by the State government and disaster management authorities. He stressed simple yet essential habits such as wearing light cotton clothing, staying hydrated with buttermilk and coconut water, and avoiding direct sun exposure during the peak afternoon hours.
“Stepping out between 12 noon and 4 p.m. should be avoided,” he noted, adding that children, elderly, and pregnant women should be careful.
Chittoor district Collector Sumit Kumar said that temperatures are already touching 38°-40° Celsius. “A few mandals — including Gangavaram, Ramakuppam, Nagari, and Vijayapuram — are recording even higher readings. While a formal heatwave has not been declared yet, officials say the situation could tighten over the next week or so,” he said.
Officials pointed out that staying out in the heat for long, without enough water, can quickly lead to exhaustion and other heat-related problems.
The India Meteorological Department forecasted that rainfall this season may be below normal due to El Niño conditions, though some showers are likely in May.
Authorities said that they arranging drinking water through borewells and tankers where needed, and keeping field staff on alert in vulnerable pockets.
Published - April 19, 2026 07:01 pm IST
